prompt: The LocalDateTime comparison gives the wrong result. What is the bug?
choices: == compares object references, not date-time values; two distinct LocalDateTime objects representing the same moment are never == even if equal; use isAfter(), isBefore(), or equals() for value comparison
LocalDateTime.of requires a ZoneId parameter to compare correctly
explanation: == on objects checks reference identity. Two separate LocalDateTime objects with the same or different values are always different references, so == is always false and useless for date comparison. Fix: use a.isAfter(b) (true here — 10:00 > 9:00), a.isBefore(b), or a.equals(b).
